Skip to main content
 
About

The AZ Awards Trophy
About the AZ Awards
The AZ Awards is AZURE’s international architecture and design competition, recognizing excellence and innovation, and showcasing the world’s best projects, products and ideas.
Now in its 16th year

Launched by AZURE in 2011, the AZ Awards has grown in size, scope and momentum to be internationally recognized for its influence in the global design and architecture spheres, and has become a significant benchmark for excellence, attracting entries from some of the most innovative firms around the world.

The AZ Awards is a unique opportunity for architects, designers, landscape architects, urban designers, students, clients and manufacturers, to receive the international exposure and acknowledgement that their exceptional work deserves.

Winners receive:
The AZ Awards trophy –one-of-a-kind trophy is re-imagined each year by a prominent designer, and produced in a limited edition exclusively for the AZ Awards
Recognition of their achievements in print and online
Global exposure through our international media partners
A complimentary press kit distribution on v2com newswire
The A+ Award bestows a CAD $5,000 prize. (If there is more than one student winner, the cash prize is split)
The AZ Awards Jury

Made up of international experts – architects, landscape architects and designers at the forefront of their professions – the AZ Awards jury convenes in March to review the submissions and select the winners.

Past jurors have included Italian furniture manufacturer Giulio Cappellini; internationally acclaimed designers Patricia Urquiola, Omer Arbel and Ini Archibong; Glenn Pushelberg and George Yabu of interior design firm Yabu Pushelberg; Craig Dykers of architecture firm Snøhetta; Los Angeles landscape architect Mia Lehrer; Nader Tehrani of Boston’s NADAAA and Japanese architect Sou Fujimoto.

See all

About AZURE
Published by Sandow and distributed primarily in North America, Azure is a leading contemporary design magazine and one of a handful of influential design magazines worldwide. In four visually stunning issues a year, we deliver coverage of architecture, interiors, product design, landscape design and urbanism. Through profiles of top architects and designers, stories about transformative projects, and trend news from major design fairs around the world, Azure anticipates the future, provides invaluable insight and shines a spotlight on the issues, ideas and people that matter. Winners and finalists of the AZ Awards are published in the AZ Awards Annual – Azure‘s Summer issue – and featured on Azure’s digital and social channels.
Want to partner with us?

Contact us

Still have questions?

Read the FAQ

Contact us at azawards@azureonline.com

The Jury

Made up of international experts – architects, landscape architects and designers at the forefront of their professions – the AZ Awards jury convenes in March to review the submissions and select the winners.

BROWSE BY
YEAR
2025 Jury

Our 2025 jury includes Rasmus Astrup, Ali McQuaid Mitchell, Simone Vingerhoets-Ziesmann and more influential thinkers and doers in the realm of global design, architecture and urbanism.

Read more

2024 Jury

Our 2024 jury includes Héctor Esrawe, Wanda Dalla Costa, Piero Lissoni and more influential thinkers and doers in the realm of global design, architecture and urbanism.

Read more

2023 Jury

Our 2023 jury includes Pascale Sablan, Lawrence Scarpa, Peter Mabeo and more influential thinkers and doers in the realm of global design, architecture and urbanism.

Read more

2022 Jury

Our 10 remarkable jurors include architects Alison Brooks and Ma Yansong, industrial designers Gabriele Chiave and Garth Roberts and interiors stars Primo Orpilla and Róisín Lafferty.

Read more

2021 Jury

Our 10 spectacular jurors include designer Patricia Urquiola, architect Sou Fujimoto and interdisciplinary designer Karim Rashid.

Read more

2020 Jury

We are excited to introduce the jurors for the AZ Awards 2020, the 10th edition of an international competition celebrating excellence in architecture and design.

Read more

2019 Jury

In March, a jury composed of five celebrated design pros convened in Toronto to evaluate the submissions for this year’s AZ Awards, which celebrate the best in architecture, design, concepts, environmental leadership and social good. The jurors selected the overall winners in 20 categories as well as dozens of finalists. Their focus was inspiring, the debate was robust and their choices – as our winners show – are nonpareil.

Read more

2018 Jury

We are excited to introduce the five jurors for the eighth annual AZ Awards, Azure’s international competition that celebrates excellence in architecture and design.

Read more

2017 Jury

This year’s illustrious jury is noted for exceptional work in the disciplines that are reflected in the AZ Awards’ categories – architecture, landscape architecture, product design, interiors, and, for the first time this year, experiential design.

Read more

2016 Jury

Our international jury was also made up of some of the industry’s heavy-hitters: architect Chris Wilkinson of WilkinsonEyre (London, U.K.), interior designer Anna Simone of Cecconi Simone (Toronto, Canada), landscape architect Thomas L. Woltz of Nelson Byrd Woltz Landscape Architects (Charlottesville, U.S.), architect John Tong of +tongtong (Toronto, Canada), and Omer Arbel of Bocci (Vancouver, Canada).

Read more

2015 Jury

The 2015 jury composed some of the very best in their fields: architect Winka Dubbeldam of Archi-Tectonics (New York, US), architect Brendan MacFarlane of Jakob + MacFarlane (Paris, France), designer Anwar Mekhayech of the Design Agency (Toronto, Canada), landscape architect Janet Rosenberg of Janet Rosenberg & Studio (Toronto, Canada) and designer Philippe Malouin (London, UK).

Read more

2014 Jury

Our esteemed jury convened in Toronto to evaluate the 652 entries to the fourth annual AZ Awards.

Read more

获奖者.text:
Skip to main content
 
About
Details
FAQ
Jury
Award Winners
Winner Celebrations
The AZ Awards Trophy
Award Winners

Each year, the prestigious AZ Awards Jury convenes in March to review the submissions, select the finalists and among them the winners of AZURE’s international competition.

The shortlisted submissions are then celebrated in June as Winner and/or People’s Choice, or Award of Merit.

Winners

Winners are chosen as the very best entries by the AZ Awards jury. These exceptional projects, products and concepts stand out among the finalists as the year’s most innovative, socially minded and sustainable design. Winners receive the one-of a kind AZ Awards trophy created by a prominent designer each year.

Meet the Winners of the 15th Annual AZ Awards!

People’s Choice

People’s Choice honourees are selected by the public through the online People’s Choice voting and represent the voters’ favourite finalists in each category.

Awards of Merit

Awards of Merit are designated to all other striking finalists shortlisted by the jury for their innovative spirit, visionary scope and overall design excellence.

Azure Magazine
Sign In
Search
314
CURRENT ISSUE

Jan/Feb 2026

#314
Jan/Feb 2026

The AZURE Houses issue returns in 2026 with stunning, innovative residential projects from Canada and around the world. Plus, we take a look at that seeming relic of the past: the mall.

FEATURES
Byoung Cho’s Sloped Roof House Meets the Ground — and the Mountain
Hevia Poblete’s Centipede House in Santiago Makes Virtue of Repetition
Studio VAARO Brings Space and Modern Character to a Toronto Heritage Home
The Art of Living: Inside Kenneth Montague’s Family Home and Gallery
In Detroit, Dash Marshall Designs a Courtyard House With No Bedrooms
The City Comes to the Mall
FIRST + FOREMOST
In Vancouver, a Coffee Date With the Late Arthur Erickson 
Atelier Carle Smooths Out the Wrinkles in a Dermatology Clinic
Two Olympics Architecture Projects Playing the Long Game
The Best of Feria Hábitat 2025, Valencia’s Annual Design Fair
3 Trends at Cersaie 2025 That Left a Lasting Impression
Meet Ourse, the Next Great Canadian Furniture Brand
A Necklace of a Landscape Wraps Around the University of Toronto
Jean Nouvel Reimagines the Fondation Cartier as a Museum in Motion
Bosch Appliances Lend a Helping Hand to a Lakeside Toronto Kitchen
Valor’s Gas Fireplaces Reimagine the Ritual of the Hearth
SPOTLIGHT: LIGHTING
Minimal Studio Illuminates a Brutalist-Inspired Supermarket with Recycled Plastic Crates
4 Striking Floor Lamps That Command Attention
4 Tiny Table Lamps Making a Big Design Statement
4 Pendant Lamps That Embrace Linearity
PLUS:
3 Recent Releases Examining Everyday Living Through Design Culture
Cancelled Monuments Move Into an L.A. Museum Exhibition
